sections.label: Section 6.250
sections.translation: Justice inclines her scales so that wisdom comes at the price of suffering . But the future , that you shall know when it occurs ; till then , leave it be — it is just as someone weeping ahead of time . Clear it will come , together with the light of dawn . Enter Clytaemestra

sections.label: Section 6.255
sections.original: πέλοιτο δ οὖν τἀπὶ τούτοισιν εὖ πρᾶξις , [*] ὡς
sections.translation: But as for what shall follow , may the issue be happy , even as she wishes , our sole guardian here , the bulwark of the Apian land , who stands nearest to our lord .

sections.label: Section 258
sections.translation: I have come , Clytaemestra , in obedience to your royal authority ; for it is fitting to do homage to the consort of a sovereign prince

sections.label: Section 260
sections.translation: when her husband ’ s throne is empty . Now whether the news you have heard is good or ill , and you do make sacrifice with hopes that herald gladness , I wish to hear ; yet , if you would keep silence , I make no complaint .

sections.label: Section 264
sections.translation: As herald of gladness , with the proverb ,

sections.label: Section 265
sections.translation: may Dawn be born from her mother Night ! You shall hear joyful news surpassing all your hopes — the Argives have taken Priam ’ s town !

sections.label: Section 268
sections.original: πῶς φῄς ; πέφευγε τοὔπος ἐξ ἀπιστίας .
sections.translation: What have you said ? The meaning of your words has escaped me , so incredible they seemed .

sections.label: Section 269
sections.original: Τροίαν Ἀχαιῶν οὖσαν : ἦ τορῶς λέγω ;
sections.translation: I said that Troy is in the hands of the Achaeans . Is my meaning clear ?

sections.label: Section 270
sections.translation: Joy steals over me , and it challenges my tears .

sections.label: Section 271
sections.original: εὖ γὰρ φρονοῦντος ὄμμα σοῦ κατηγορεῖ .
sections.translation: Sure enough , for your eye betrays your loyal heart .

sections.label: Section 272
sections.original: τί γὰρ τὸ πιστόν ; [*] ἔστι τῶνδέ σοι τέκμαρ ;
sections.translation: What then is the proof ? Have you evidence of this ?

sections.label: Section 273
sections.original: ἔστιν : τί δ οὐχί ; μὴ δολώσαντος θεοῦ .
sections.translation: I have , indeed ; unless some god has played me false .

sections.label: Section 274
sections.original: πότερα δ ὀνείρων φάσματ εὐπιθῆ [*] σέβεις ;
sections.translation: Do you believe the persuasive visions of dreams ?

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: οὐ δόξαν ἂν λάβοιμι βριζούσης φρενός .
sections.translation: I would not heed the fancies of a slumbering brain .

sections.label: Section 276
sections.original: ἀλλ ἦ σ ἐπίανέν τις ἄπτερος φάτις ;
sections.translation: But can it be some pleasing rumor that has fed your hopes ?

sections.label: Section 277
sections.original: παιδὸς νέας ὣς κάρτ ἐμωμήσω φρένας .
sections.translation: Truly you scorn my understanding as if it were a child ’ s .

sections.label: Section 278
sections.original: ποίου χρόνου δὲ καὶ πεπόρθηται πόλις ;
sections.translation: But at what time was the city destroyed ?

sections.label: Section 279
sections.original: τῆς νῦν τεκούσης φῶς τόδ εὐφρόνης λέγω .
sections.translation: In the night , I say , that has but now given birth to this day here .

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: καὶ τίς τόδ ἐξίκοιτ ἂν ἀγγέλων τάχος ;
sections.translation: And what messenger could reach here with such speed ?

sections.label: Section 281
sections.translation: Hephaestus , from Ida speeding forth his brilliant blaze . Beacon passed beacon on to us by courier-flame : Ida , to the Hermaean crag in Lemnos ; to the mighty blaze upon the island succeeded , third ,

sections.label: Section 285
sections.translation: the summit of Athos sacred to Zeus ; and , soaring high aloft so as to leap across the sea , the flame , travelling joyously onward in its strength ⋯ the pinewood torch , its golden-beamed light , as another sun , passing the message on to the watchtowers of Macistus .

sections.label: Section 290
sections.original: ὁ δ οὔτι μέλλων οὐδ ἀφρασμόνως ὕπνῳ
sections.translation: He , delaying not nor carelessly overcome by sleep , did not neglect his part as messenger . Far over Euripus ’ stream came the beacon-light and signalled to the watchmen on Messapion . They , kindling a heap of

sections.label: Section 295
sections.translation: withered heather , lit up their answering blaze and sped the message on . The flame , now gathering strength and in no way dimmed , like a radiant moon overleaped the plain of Asopus to Cithaeron ’ s ridges , and roused another relay of missive fire .

sections.label: Section 300
sections.translation: Nor did the warders there disdain the far-flung light , but made a blaze higher than their commands . Across Gorgopus ’ water shot the light , reached the mount of Aegiplanctus , and urged the ordinance of fire to make no delay .

sections.label: Section 305
sections.translation: Kindling high with unstinted force a mighty beard of flame , they sped it forward so that , as it blazed , it passed even the headland that looks upon the Saronic gulf ; until it swooped down when it reached the lookout , near to our city , upon the peak of Arachnaeus ; and

sections.label: Section 310
sections.original: κἄπειτ Ἀτρειδῶν ἐς τόδε σκήπτει στέγος
sections.translation: next upon this roof of the Atreidae it leapt , this very fire not undescended from the Idaean flame . Such are the torch-bearers I have arranged , completing the course in succession one to the other ; and the victor is he who ran both first and last . [*]

sections.label: Section 315
sections.original: τέκμαρ τοιοῦτο σύμβολόν τε σοὶ λέγω
sections.translation: This is the kind of proof and token I give you , the message of my husband from Troy to me .

sections.label: Section 317
sections.original: θεοῖς μὲν αὖθις , ὦ γύναι , προσεύξομαι .
sections.translation: Lady , my prayers of thanksgiving to the gods I will offer soon . But as I would like to hear and satisfy my wonder at your tale straight through to the end , so may you tell it yet again .

sections.label: Section 320
sections.original: Τροίαν Ἀχαιοὶ τῇδ ἔχουσ ἐν ἡμέρᾳ .
sections.translation: This day the Achaeans hold Troy . Within the town there sounds loud , I believe , a clamor of voices which will not blend . Pour vinegar and oil into the same vessel and you will say that , as foes , they keep apart ; so the cries of vanquished and victors greet the ear ,

sections.label: Section 325
sections.translation: distinct as their fortunes are diverse . Those , flung upon the corpses of their husbands and their brothers , children upon the bodies of their aged fathers who gave them life , bewail from lips no longer free the death of their dearest ones , while these —

sections.label: Section 330
sections.original: τοὺς δ αὖτε νυκτίπλαγκτος ἐκ μάχης πόνος
sections.translation: a night of restless toil after battle sets them down famished to break their fast on such fare as the town affords ; not faring according to rank , but as each man has drawn his lot by chance .

sections.label: Section 335
sections.translation:
And even now they are quartered in the captured Trojan homes , delivered from the frosts and dew of the naked sky , and like happy men will sleep all the night without a guard .
Now if they keep clear of guilt towards the gods of the town — those of the conquered land — and towards their shrines ,

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: οὔ τἂν ἑλόντες [*] αὖθις ἀνθαλοῖεν [*] ἄν .
sections.translation: the captors shall not be made captives in their turn . Only may no mad impulse first assail the army , overmastered by greed , to pillage what they should not ! For to win safe passage home they need to travel back the other length of their double course .

sections.label: Section 345
sections.original: θεοῖς δ ἀναμπλάκητος εἰ μόλοι στρατός ,
sections.translation: But even if , without having offended the gods , our troops should reach home , the grievous suffering of the dead might still remain awake — if no fresh disaster transpires . These are my woman ’ s words ; but may the good prevail clearly for all to see !

sections.label: Section 350
sections.original: πολλῶν γὰρ ἐσθλῶν τήνδ [*] ὄνησιν εἱλόμην .
sections.translation: For , choosing thus , I have chosen the enjoyment of many a blessing .

sections.label: Section 351
sections.original: γύναι , κατ ἄνδρα σώφρον εὐφρόνως λέγεις .
sections.translation: Lady , you speak as wisely as a prudent man . And , for my part , now that I have listened to your certain proofs , I prepare to address due prayers of thanksgiving to the gods ; for a success has been achieved that well repays the toil .

sections.label: Section 355
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ βασιλεῦ καὶ νὺξ φιλία
sections.translation: Hail , sovereign Zeus , and you kindly Night , you who have given us great glory , you who cast your meshed snare upon the towered walls of Troy , so that neither old nor young could overleap

sections.label: Section 360
sections.translation: the huge enslaving net of all-conquering Destruction . Great Zeus it is , lord of host and guest , whom I revere — he has brought this to pass . He long kept his bow bent against Alexander

sections.label: Section 365
sections.original: μήτε πρὸ καιροῦ μήθ ὑπὲρ ἄστρων
sections.translation: until his bolt would neither fall short of the mark nor , flying beyond the stars , be launched in vain .

sections.label: Section 1.367
sections.translation: The stroke of Zeus they may call it ; his hand can be traced there . As he determines , so he acts . Someone said

sections.label: Section 1.370
sections.translation:
that the gods do not trouble themselves to remember mortals who trample underfoot the grace of things not to be touched . But that man was impious !
Now it stands revealed !

sections.label: Section 1.375
sections.translation: The penalty for reckless crime is ruin when men breathe a spirit of pride above just measure , because their mansions teem with more abundance than is good for them . But let there be such wealth as brings no distress , enough to satisfy

sections.label: Section 1.380
sections.translation: a sensible man . For riches do not protect the man who in wantonness has kicked the mighty altar of Justice into obscurity .

sections.label: Section 1.385
sections.translation: Perverse Temptation , the overmastering child of designing Destruction , drives men on ; and every remedy is futile . His evil is not hidden ; it shines forth , a baleful gleam .

sections.label: Section 1.390
sections.translation: Like base metal beneath the touchstone ’ s rub , when tested he shows the blackness of his grain ( for he is like a child who chases a winged bird )

sections.label: Section 1.395
sections.translation:
and upon his people he brings a taint against which there is no defence . No god listens to his prayers . The man associated with such deeds , him they destroy in his unrighteousness .
And such was Paris , who came

sections.label: Section 1.400
sections.translation: to the house of the sons of Atreus and dishonoured the hospitality of his host by stealing away a wedded wife .

sections.label: Section 2.405
sections.translation: But she , bequeathing to her people the clang of shield and spear and army of fleets , and bringing to Ilium destruction in place of dowry , with light step she passed through the gates — daring a deed undareable . Then loud wailed the seers of the house crying ,

sections.label: Section 2.410
sections.original: “ ἰὼ ἰὼ δῶμα δῶμα καὶ πρόμοι ,
sections.translation: Alas , alas , for the home , the home , and for the princes ! Alas for the husband ’ s bed and the impress of her form so dear ! He sits apart in the anguish of his grief , silent , dishonored but making no reproach . In his yearning for her who sped beyond the sea ,

sections.label: Section 2.415
sections.translation: a phantom will seem to be lord of the house . The grace of fair-formed statues is hateful to him ; and in the hunger of his eyes all loveliness is departed .

sections.label: Section 2.420
sections.translation: Mournful apparitions come to him in dreams , bringing only vain joy ; for vainly , whenever in his imagination a man sees delights ,

sections.label: Section 2.425
sections.translation: straightaway the vision , slipping through his arms , is gone , winging its flight along the paths of sleep . Such are the sorrows at hearth and home , but here are sorrows surpassing these ; and at large , in every house of all who went forth together from the land of Hellas ,

sections.label: Section 2.430
sections.translation: unbearable grief is seen . Many things pierce the heart . Each knows whom he sent forth . But to the home of each come

sections.label: Section 2.435
sections.translation: urns and ashes [*] , not living men .

sections.label: Section 3.437
sections.translation: Ares barters the bodies of men for gold ; he holds his balance in the contest of the spear ; and

sections.label: Section 3.440
sections.translation: back from Ilium to their loved ones he sends a heavy dust passed through his burning , a dust cried over with plenteous tears , in place of men sending well made urns with ashes .

sections.label: Section 3.445
sections.translation: So they lament , praising now this one : How skilled in battle ! now that one : Fallen nobly in the carnage , — for another ’ s wife — some mutter in secret , and

sections.label: Section 3.450
sections.original: ζει : φθονερὸν δ ὑπ ἄλγος ἕρ-
sections.translation: grief charged with resentment spreads stealthily against the sons of Atreus , champions in the strife . But there far from home , around the city ’ s walls , those in their beauty ’ s bloom have graves in Ilium —

sections.label: Section 3.455
sections.translation: the enemy ’ s soil has covered its conquerors .

sections.label: Section 3.456
sections.original: βαρεῖα δ ἀστῶν φάτις ξὺν κότῳ :
sections.translation: Dangerous is a people ’ s voice charged with wrath — it acts as a curse of publicly ratified doom .
